message:
fcp-core/policy: gate the unused direct-zone preview surface behind cfg(test)

`crates/fcp-core/src/policy.rs` exposed two parallel "preview" APIs
through the wildcard re-export in `crates/fcp-core/src/lib.rs:78`:

1. `preview_policy_bundles` (line 1937) plus
   `PolicyPreviewSample` / `PolicyPreviewReport` / `PolicyPreviewEntry`
   / `PolicyPreviewDecision` / `PolicyPreviewDelta` /
   `PolicyPreviewSummary` / `PolicyPreviewError`. This is the
   canonical public preview API — `Grep PolicyPreviewSample crates`
   shows fwc/src/policy_cmd.rs at lines 17, 754, 755, 758, 764, 767
   loading samples from disk and feeding them through it, plus the
   in-module tests.

2. `preview_policy_changes` (line 3222) plus `PreviewOutcome`,
   `PreviewDeltaEntry`, `PreviewSummary`, `PreviewResult`,
   `PreviewInput`. This is a parallel surface ("direct zone policy
   preview, would-allow without bundle resolution" per its own
   section comment) that no production code imports — `Grep
   "PreviewInput\|PreviewResult\|preview_policy_changes" crates`
   returns only this file, and no owner-crate re-export
   (`fcp-kernel/src/lib.rs`, `fcp-policy/src/lib.rs`,
   `fcp-evidence/src/lib.rs`) lists any of these names. The only
   callers are the four `direct_preview_*` tests below in this
   file's `tests` module (this file's lines 5364, 5405, 5442, 5457).

Originally added in commit 2f4146c5 (2026-03-01, "policy preview
API and 500+ new tests"); the seven weeks since are enough that any
intended consumer would have shown up by now. The duplication was
visible to callers as two near-identical type families
(`Preview*` vs `PolicyPreview*`) shipped behind the same wildcard
re-export, which is exactly the kind of API-evolution surface the
FCP3 owner-crate carve-out
(`docs/FCP3_Semantic_Ownership_Inventory.md`) is trying to shrink.

Fix:

* Add `#[cfg(test)]` to each item in the "Direct Zone Policy Preview"
  section: `PreviewOutcome` enum (3140), `From<Decision> for
  PreviewOutcome` impl (3147), `PreviewDeltaEntry` (3158),
  `PreviewSummary` (3175), `PreviewResult` (3190), `PreviewInput`
  (3203), `preview_policy_changes` fn (3222). All seven now compile
  only in test builds. Production lib build no longer ships them
  through the `pub use policy::*` wildcard.
* Inline section comment records the rationale and the
  one-line-per-item path back to `pub` if a real consumer
  materialises later.

Why `#[cfg(test)]` instead of `pub(crate)`:

The first attempt was a straight `pub` → `pub(crate)` downgrade,
which compiled but produced six new lib-build warnings of the form
`enum PreviewOutcome is never used` / `function preview_policy_changes
is never used`. Dead-code analysis runs against the lib binary and
does not see test-only consumers, so `pub(crate)` items used only
from `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` are flagged dead. Under the
workspace's `clippy -- -D warnings` policy that turns into a hard
build failure. `#[cfg(test)]` is the honest answer: these items are
genuinely test-only today, and gating them on `#[cfg(test)]` makes
that explicit, removes them from non-test builds entirely, and
keeps dead-code analysis quiet without an `#[allow(dead_code)]`
suppression that would just hide the same situation.

No semantic change to any caller. The `From<Decision> for
PreviewOutcome` impl gets gated alongside its target type so no
orphan-impl warning fires.

Verified locally with rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-cc1:
* `cargo test -p fcp-core --lib` → 3918 passed, 0 failed (the
  `direct_preview_*` tests in this file still run because they
  compile under `#[cfg(test)]`)
* `cargo build -p fcp-core --lib` → no fcp-core warnings (the
  `task_id` warning shown is the pre-existing
  asupersync upstream issue, not from this commit)
* `cargo clippy -p fcp-core --lib -- -D warnings` → clean

Audit notes for the rest of the kernel/core boundary, all clean:

* `validate_canonical_id` (capability.rs:55) — pub, used by
  fcp-manifest:1487 (lib code) and fcp-host. Legitimately public.
* `compute_policy_bundle_hash` (policy.rs:280),
  `diff_policy_bundles` (policy.rs:912),
  `preview_policy_bundles` (policy.rs:1937),
  `simulate_policy_decision` (policy.rs:2266) — all used by
  fwc/src/policy_cmd.rs. Legitimately public.
* `pattern_matches` (policy.rs:2744) — already correctly
  `pub(crate)`. No change.
* `apply_sanitizer_receipts`, `apply_flow_approvals`,
  `approval_token_object_id`, `sanitizer_receipt_object_id` —
  module-private free functions. No change.
* `TypedLifecycleRecord` / `AnyLifecycleRecord`
  (lifecycle.rs:589, 770) — pub struct with private `inner` field.
  Encapsulated correctly.
* `ZoneBound<T>` (capability.rs:583) — pub struct with private
  fields plus `into_inner_unchecked` marked `#[doc(hidden)]`.
  Encapsulated correctly.
* All `pub fn new` / `from_static` / `as_str` on the ID types are
  legitimate constructor/accessor surface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
